ddls:
CREATE TABLE "packages" (
  "package_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each package */,
  "directory" TEXT /* Directory path where the package is installed */,
  "type" INTEGER /* Type of package (e.g., config, lib, exe) */,
  "pkg_src" INTEGER /* Source of the package (e.g., local, upstream) */,
  "active" INTEGER /* Whether the package is active or not */,
  "path_env_var" TEXT /* Environment variable path for the package */,
  "haddock" INTEGER /* Whether haddock documentation is available */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the package */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the package was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the package was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("package_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_packages_pkg_src FOREIGN KEY ("pkg_src") REFERENCES package_sources ("source_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_packages_type FOREIGN KEY ("type") REFERENCES package_types ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "package_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each package type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the package type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the package type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "package_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each package source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the package source */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the package source */,
  "url" TEXT /* URL of the package source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "dependencies" (
  "dependency_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each dependency */,
  "package_id" INTEGER /* ID of the package that depends on another package */,
  "dependent_package_id" INTEGER /* ID of the package that is depended on */,
  "dependency_type" TEXT /* Type of dependency (e.g., build, runtime) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("dependency_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_dependencies_dependent_package_id FOREIGN KEY ("dependent_package_id") REFERENCES packages ("package_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_dependencies_package_id FOREIGN KEY ("package_id") REFERENCES packages ("package_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version */,
  "package_id" INTEGER /* ID of the package that the version belongs to */,
  "version_name" TEXT /* Name of the version */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Release date of the version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_versions_package_id FOREIGN KEY ("package_id") REFERENCES packages ("package_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "environment_variables" (
  "env_var_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each environment variable */,
  "package_id" INTEGER /* ID of the package that the environment variable belongs to */,
  "env_var_name" TEXT /* Name of the environment variable */,
  "env_var_value" TEXT /* Value of the environment variable */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("env_var_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_environment_variables_package_id FOREIGN KEY ("package_id") REFERENCES packages ("package_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user's role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role that the permission belongs to */,
  "permission_name" TEXT /* Name of the permission */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_permissions_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "package_id" INTEGER /* ID of the package being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the package */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Timestamp when the package was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_package_id FOREIGN KEY ("package_id") REFERENCES packages ("package_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "package_downloads" (
  "download_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each download event */,
  "package_id" INTEGER /* ID of the package being downloaded */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user downloading the package */,
  "download_date" TEXT /* Timestamp when the package was downloaded */,
  "download_location" TEXT /* Location where the package was downloaded */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("download_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_package_downloads_package_id FOREIGN KEY ("package_id") REFERENCES packages ("package_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_package_downloads_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "teams" (
  "team_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each team */,
  "team_name" TEXT /* Name of the team */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the team */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("team_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "team_memberships" (
  "membership_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each membership */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "team_id" INTEGER /* ID of the team */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user in the team */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("membership_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_team_memberships_team_id FOREIGN KEY ("team_id") REFERENCES teams ("team_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_team_memberships_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "package_comments" (
  "comment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each comment */,
  "package_id" INTEGER /* ID of the package being commented on */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user making the comment */,
  "comment_text" TEXT /* Text of the comment */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the comment was made */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("comment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_package_comments_package_id FOREIGN KEY ("package_id") REFERENCES packages ("package_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_package_comments_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
